channel.title: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Industrial Control Systems (ICS) — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Industrial Control Systems (ICS) (icscert).

image.title: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Industrial Control Systems (ICS) — recent CVEs

item.title: CVE-2026-28698 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere in Panduit IntraVUE by Pronetiqs
item.description: Pronetiqs IntraVUE versions 3.2.1a14 and prior have an exposure of sensitive system information to an unauthorized control sphere vulnerability which could expose the underlying host/share filesystem.

item.title: CVE-2026-50044 · 7.6 HIGH — Inadequate Encryption Strength in Panduit IntraVUE by Pronetiqs
item.description: Pronetiqs IntraVUE versions 3.2.1a14 and prior have an inadequate encryption strength vulnerability which could allow an attacker to steal admin credentials via weak hash or a pass-the-hash attack.

item.title: CVE-2026-44955 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere in Panduit IntraVUE by Pronetiqs
item.description: Pronetiqs IntraVUE versions 3.2.1a14 and prior have an exposure of sensitive system information to an unauthorized control sphere vulnerability which could allow for asset discovery by unauthenticated users.

item.title: CVE-2026-42933 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Unintended Proxy or Intermediary in Panduit IntraVUE by Pronetiqs
item.description: Pronetiqs IntraVUE versions 3.2.1a14 and prior have an unintended proxy or intermediary vulnerability which could allow an attacker to use an active proxy, which would bypass OT segmentation.

item.title: CVE-2026-40430 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Plaintext Storage of a Password in Panduit IntraVUE by Pronetiqs
item.description: Pronetiqs IntraVUE Versions 3.2.1a14 and prior have a plaintext storage of a password vulnerability that could expose cleartext credentials through the API.

item.title: CVE-2026-16002 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — Out-of-bounds Read in MZ Automation lib60870
item.description: The affected product is vulnerable to an Out-of-bounds read, which may allow an attacker to crash the parsing process and cause a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-50032 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — NULL Pointer Dereference in MZ Automation libIEC61850
item.description: A NULL pointer dereference in the MMS Write Named Variable List handler, which may allow a network adjacent attacker to crash the server by sending a WriteRequest with an empty listOfData field.

item.title: CVE-2026-50103 · 7.1 HIGH — Improper Handling of Syntactically Invalid Structure in MZ Automation libIEC61850
item.description: A NULL pointer dereference in the L2 GOOSE and R-GOOSE shared parser, which may allow a network-adjacent attacker to crash a subscribing application by sending a crafted GOOSE frame containing a malformed TLV value.

item.title: CVE-2026-49035 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Stack-based Buffer Overflow in MZ Automation libIEC61850
item.description: The affected product is vulnerable to a heap-based buffer overflow via a crafted MMS Initiate request. Remote code execution (RCE) has been demonstrated when ASLR is disabled; memory corruption or denial of service may occur in configurations where A…

item.title: CVE-2026-50039 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Stack-based Buffer Overflow in MZ Automation libIEC61850
item.description: The affected product is vulnerable to a stack-based buffer overflow, which may allow an attacker to cause a memory corruption via a Read Request.

item.title: CVE-2026-11889 · 7.1 HIGH — SALTO ProAccess Space Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
item.description: SALTO ProAccess Space software using the tenancy feature / logical partition is vulnerable to a privilege escalation attack that could allow an authorized attacker to access any space managed by the affected product.

item.title: CVE-2026-61378 · 6.8 MEDIUM — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Divide By Zero
item.description: A divide-by-zero vulnerability in the Productivity Suite allows a local attacker to cause a division by zero leading to a system crash.

item.title: CVE-2026-60073 · 5.9 MEDIUM — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Out-of-bounds Read
item.description: An out-of-bounds read in the Productivity Suite allows a physical attacker to control the length of data sent to a USB device. This can lead to a system crash or disclosure of kernel memory.

item.title: CVE-2026-57896 · 6.9 MEDIUM — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Out-of-bounds Read
item.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the Productivity Suite allows a local attacker to trigger kernel memory corruption by sending a crafted IOCTL request. This could lead to limited information disclosure or disruption of the affected product.

item.title: CVE-2026-60140 · 6.9 MEDIUM — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Out-of-bounds Read
item.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the Productivity Suite allows a local attacker to trigger kernel memory corruption by sending a crafted IOCTL request. This can lead to exposing sensitive information or causing the affected product to become un…

item.title: CVE-2026-61389 · 7.3 HIGH — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Out-of-bounds Write
item.description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Productivity Suite allows a local attacker to trigger kernel memory corruption via a crafted IOCTL request, potentially resulting in privilege escalation or system instability.

item.title: CVE-2026-60063 · 7.3 HIGH — AutomationDirect Productivity Suite Out-of-bounds Write
item.description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Productivity Suite allows a local attacker to trigger kernel memory corruption via a crafted IOCTL request, potentially resulting in privilege escalation or system instability.

item.title: CVE-2026-15352 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — NASA Core Flight System (cFS) Health & Safety (HS) Application NULL Pointer Dereference
item.description: A vulnerability exists in the Health & Safety (HS) application of NASA's Core Flight System (cFS). The flaw allows the application to crash via segmentation fault when processing a routine Housekeeping Telemetry request, leading to denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-14480 · 9.9 CRITICAL — OpenPLC v3 External Control of File Name or Path
item.description: OpenPLC Runtime v3 contains an authenticated arbitrary file write vulnerability in the legacy web UI program‑upload workflow. The application stores an attacker‑supplied filename (prog_file) directly into the Programs.File database field and later us…

item.title: CVE-2026-44383 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Electrique charging station backend Insufficient Session Expiration
item.description: Multiple connections to the backend using the same charging station ID are allowed, which could allow an attacker to deploy multiple instances of malicious OCPP clients to overwhelm the backend.

item.title: CVE-2026-42952 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Electrique charging station backend Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
item.description: Previously, there was no throttling on repeated authentication attempts to the charging station backend, which could allow an attacker to execute a denial-of-service attack.

item.title: CVE-2026-20744 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Hydro-Québec Le Circuit Electrique charging station backend Improper Access Control
item.description: The charging station websocket endpoint accepts connections without proper authentication, which could lead to privilege escalation.

item.title: CVE-2026-38059 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — ST Engineering iDirect iQ-Series Terminals Missing authentication for critical function
item.description: The iDirect iQ200 exposes the /api/identity and /api/ REST API endpoints without authentication. An unauthenticated attacker with network access can retrieve sensitive device information including the serial number, Device ID (DID), Terminal Private…

item.title: CVE-2026-38057 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — ST Engineering iDirect iQ-Series Terminals Cross-Site request forgery
item.description: The iDirect iQ200 does not validate CSRF tokens on state-changing API endpoints after authentication. The /api/reboot endpoint accepts POST requests authenticated solely by a session cookie that lacks the SameSite attribute. A remote attacker can hos…

item.title: CVE-2026-49033 · 8.4 HIGH — Stack-Based Buffer Overflow in Labcenter Proteus
item.description: The application contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that can be exploited by an attacker to execute arbitrary code.

item.title: CVE-2026-42958 · 8.4 HIGH — Use After Free in Labcenter Proteus
item.description: The application contains a use-after-free vulnerability that can be exploited to cause memory corruption while parsing specially crafted files. This could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code in the context of the current process.

item.title: CVE-2026-42953 · 8.4 HIGH — Out-of-bounds write in Labcenter Proteus
item.description: The application contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability that can be exploited by an attacker to cause the program to write data past the end of an allocated memory buffer. This can lead to arbitrary code execution.

item.title: CVE-2026-54477 · 5.4 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Gardyn IoT Hub Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
item.description: The admin panel lacks standard security headers, enabling clickjacking and cross-site scripting attacks.

item.title: CVE-2026-55726 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Gardyn IoT Hub Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
item.description: The Azure Blob Storage container used for Gardyn device logs is publicly listable without authentication. A malicious user would be able to access any device log file available in the blob storage container.

item.title: CVE-2026-13768 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gardyn IoT Hub Use of Hard-coded Credentials
item.description: Gardyn devices expose a privileged iothubowner key. Access to this key will allow a malicious user to invoke an IoTHub Registry Manager function which returns connection information for all Gardyn Home Kit and Studio devices. Access to this key also…

item.title: CVE-2026-13743 · 3.3 LOW — Improper verification of cryptographic signature in CubeSpace CW0057 Reaction Wheel
item.description: CubeSpace CW0057 Reaction Wheel firmware versions prior to 5.0.20 are vulnerable to an Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature vulnerability. This could allow an attacker with physical access to the product to upload arbitrary malicious firm…

item.title: CVE-2026-50110 · 9.3 CRITICAL — Use of Hard-coded Credentials in StoneFly Storage Concentrator
item.description: Storage Concentrator (SC & SCVM) contains hardcoded credentials for numerous internal services embedded within a configuration file. While the credentials are stored in an encoded format, the encoding can be reversed to plaintext. The exposed credent…

item.title: CVE-2026-56413 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — OS Command Injection in StoneFly Storage Concentrator
item.description: Storage Concentrator (SC & SCVM) contains a command injection vulnerability in the ms_service.pl service, which listens on TCP port 9000 by default and accepts custom network packets to perform device actions. An unauthenticated remote attacker can s…

item.title: CVE-2026-56415 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — OS Command Injection in StoneFly Storage Concentrator
item.description: Storage Concentrator (SC & SCVM) contains a command injection vulnerability within the debug.pl script that is reachable without authentication. A remote attacker can submit a specially crafted HTTP request containing a malicious payload that is proc…

item.title: CVE-2026-55721 · 9.3 CRITICAL 🌩️ — SQL Injection in StoneFly Storage Concentrator
item.description: Storage Concentrator (SC & SCVM) is vulnerable to SQL injection through cookie values processed by the login.pl and debug.pl scripts. The cookie value is incorporated directly into database queries without adequate sanitization, allowing an unauthent…

item.title: CVE-2026-50040 · 6.1 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Cross-site Scripting in StoneFly Storage Concentrator
item.description: Storage Concentrator (SC & SCVM) is vulnerable to reflected cross-site scripting due to unsanitized content being echoed back in 404 error pages. An attacker can craft a malicious URL that, when visited by an authenticated user, causes arbitrary scri…

item.title: CVE-2026-50003 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — OFFIS DCMTK Toolkit Path Traversal
item.description: A malicious or compromised server can make a DCMTK client using bit-preserving C-GET storage mode write files outside the chosen output directory, using both relative (../) paths and absolute paths.

item.title: CVE-2026-50254 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — OFFIS DCMTK Toolkit Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
item.description: An unauthenticated remote attacker can repeatedly send a single crafted connection request to leak memory. Against storescp in its default single-process mode, memory grows quickly and the service is eventually killed, after which it stops accepting…

item.title: CVE-2026-35505 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — OFFIS DCMTK Toolkit Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
item.description: An unauthenticated remote attacker can repeatedly send crafted connection requests to leak memory. In single-process deployments the memory grows until the service is killed and the port stops responding until restart.

item.title: CVE-2026-52868 · 8.8 HIGH 🌩️ — OFFIS DCMTK Toolkit Path Traversal
item.description: An unauthenticated attacker can read worklist records from a directory outside the intended per-AE worklist storage area. In a multi-area deployment, this can cross departmental or clinic data separation.

item.title: CVE-2026-44628 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — OFFIS DCMTK Toolkit Type Confusion
item.description: An unauthenticated attacker can crash the worklist server with a single crafted query when the server has a valid Called AE Title / storage directory, the expected lockfile, and at least one matching worklist record.

item.title: CVE-2026-13207 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Frangoteam FUXA SCADA/HMI Authentication Bypass by Spoofing
item.description: FUXA versions 1.3.1 and prior contain an authentication bypass vulnerability via dot-segment path normalization in the REST API. The API router fails to normalize dot-segment sequences before applying authentication middleware, allowing unauthenticat…

item.title: CVE-2026-56414 · 8.6 HIGH — H.VIEW HV-500S6 IP Camera Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
item.description: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras certificate-related upload interfaces allow authenticated users to store arbitrary file content to fixed, persistent filesystem locations without validating file type, structure, or size. This design omissi…

item.title: CVE-2026-55975 · 8.6 HIGH — H.VIEW HV-500S6 IP Camera OS Command Injection
item.description: A vulnerability exists in H.View IP cameras that could allow an authenticated user to supply unsanitized XML fields to the device's certificate generation interface, which are incorporated into a backend certificate creation command without proper in…

item.title: CVE-2026-31928 · 9.3 CRITICAL — Daktronics Controller Firmware Use of Hard-coded Credentials
item.description: The DMP-5000 devices are shipped with a default administrative web account with weak authentication controls, which are not required to be changed during initial configuration or operation. Using these accounts provides full system access.

item.title: CVE-2026-33560 · 8.4 HIGH — Daktronics Controller Firmware Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
item.description: The DMP-5000 file service exposes authenticated arbitrary file upload functionality. There are exposed endpoints which allows authenticated users to upload files of any type without validation. No file extension filtering or content inspection is enf…

item.title: CVE-2026-28701 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Daktronics Controller Firmware Path Traversal
item.description: Various versions of Daktronics Controller Firmware could allow authenticated and unauthenticated remote users to escape the intended directory and enumerate arbitrary file system paths.

item.title: CVE-2026-9222 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Setracker2 Children's Smartwatch Ecosystem Use of password hash instead of password for authentication
item.description: Setracker2 Android Companion App com.tgelec.setracker versions 3.1.5 and prior only require the password hash when authenticating with backend services from the client. This could allow an attacker, who knows the hash, to authenticate and gain full a…

item.title: CVE-2026-9221 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — Setracker2 Children's Smartwatch Ecosystem Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
item.description: The Setracker2 Android Companion App (com.tgelec.setracker) versions 3.1.5 and earlier uses MD5 to generate a request signature for authenticating communications between the mobile client and the backend REST API. Attackers could potentially reverse…

item.title: CVE-2026-40702 · 9.4 CRITICAL 🌩️ — EVoke Systems EVoke CSMS Missing Authentication for Critical Function
item.description: WebSocket endpoints lack proper authentication mechanisms, enabling attackers to impersonate charging stations. As a result, attackers can exploit this weakness to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions. Given that…
